mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production accelerates while housing starts decelerate indicates supply-side overcapacity outpacing domestic demand, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM while HOUST falls > 2% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During an early tightening cycle, rising consumer prices combined with increasing policy rates trigger a repricing of inflation expectations, which increases the equity risk premium and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25%
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in real retail and food services sales reflects sustained consumer demand, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of months with positive RSAFS growth, or the rolling Spearman correlation with 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above 115.0, it signals peak capacity utilization that historically precedes margin compression due to input cost inflation, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO crosses 115.0, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k MoM and UNRATE falls > 0.2%
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with rising housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, which lifts corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq growth > 2.5% and HOUST rises > 5% MoM
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the combined demand signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.
